title: Difference between TC3XX and TC4XX - Details

content.text: This page is to summarize the difference between Aurix 2nd 3rd generation Chips

rows: Big changes to support SOTA Flash handling - allows two process RWW capability Allows Swap A/B in all flash banks increased write burst operation ........
Many small changes in almost all peripherals
ADC SCU PORTS QSPI xSPI -newly introduced
SCR handling is changes and introduced new stand by modes Many change sin Firmware
HSPHY HSSL Small changes in GETH
Small changes in almost all Safety areas. Main part in BUS MPU and peripheral protection
Introduced very important module for Gateway features - DRE

Changed TriCore architecture from version TC1.6 to TC1.8, with a new pipeline architecture Backwards compatibility towards TriCore ™ TC1.6 instructions is maintained - which means we can use the existing compiler used in TC3xx New PROT and APU concept. These replace the ENDINIT functions and bus MPU Impact on Memory protection concept New registers have been added, including for virtualization support and new PROT and APU configurations

Each Host CPU has 2 PFLASH Banks attached by point to point interface Supports SOTA A/B image swapping, programming, and checking without any performance impact The CPU of the CSRM (Cyber Security Real-time Module) Module has its own PFLASH Bank with read and write capability independent from the Host CPU PFLASH Banks Now possible 2 parallel Flash operations through 2 independent command interfaces without "time slicing" of control resources Each command interface has a set of allocated memories Improved PFLASH Write Burst operation which now comprises 512 Byte = 16 x 32 Byte No inter sector Erase and Program disturbs Separate User Configuration Blocks for Host Application in DFLASH (HOST) and for Security Application in DFLASH (CSRM) Faster Error Correction Code in PFLASH DFLASH Logical Sector Size is now 2 KB Removed Complement Sensing Mode Added the Abort operation to terminate immediately an operation which cannot be resumed anymore Performance Improvement Reduced Program Flash Erase Time per logical sector t SECTOR_ERP Reduced Data Flash Erase Time per logical sector t SECTOR_ERDM Reduced Program Flash access delay t PF Reduced Data Flash access delay t DF Increased Program Flash Program Time throughput Increased Data Flash Endurance for EEPROM and CSRM sectors

Added a lockstep DMA The DMA implements a lockstep mechanism using a redundant checker core for safety Added Virtualization support Allow DMA resource partitions to be assigned to a virtual machine and/or protection set Modified implementation of DMA Resource Partitions (RP) Increased number of DMA Resource Partitions to 16 SRI performance improvements: Added SRI burst requests support for burst length 8 (that is 64 B).

AURIX TC4xx LMU forms building block of the sub system that implements the function EMEM retaining its AURIX TC3xx features Changed access to LMU only through SRI (was accessed via BBB, SRI, SEP and trace) Changed size of the sixteen address ranges from to 64 byte increments (was 32 byte increments) The LMU instances changed to 512 KB (was 256 KB) of SRAM Added term Access Protection Units (APUs) to denote the memory access protection for the sixteen address ranges of SRAM memory. Added PROT mechanism for LMU memory protection (was ENDINIT).

Compared to the AURIX TC2xx/TC3xx HSM, the AURIX TC4xx. CSRM is a new block, part of the cybersecurity cluster (CS) containing PKC, RNG, CSCU (CS Control Unit) and the CSROM. All symmetric cryptographic functions are moved and are part of the functional block CSS. The cyber security cluster (CS) is the combination of all modules and functions that in sum realize the cyber security functionality. A CPUcs is associated to the CS cluster that has been changed from an ARM core to an TriCore version 1.8 that results in a different instruction set and a higher computation performance. Besides the CPUcs the DMA has been added as a secondary data movement engine. HSM moved all data over the SPB bus. In CSRM the main data is exchanged among CPUcs , DMA, CSS and associated protected resources of other functional blocks through the fast SRI crossbar. The name of the boot firmware formerly BOS has been changed to CSRM_FW and coexist in these device generation with the SYS_FW of the host side. ..................................

The Cyber Security Satellite (CSS) is a functional block that implements security functions. The CSS includes a SRI slave interface with a set of special function registers (SFRs). The CSS is a completely new module in AURIX TC4xx devices Augmented Symmetric Cryptographic Features: CSS contains additional symmetric ciphers , longer key lengths (128, 192, 256 bit) and more modes of operation (up to 3 AES , SM4 and ChaCha20 algorithms). Asymmetric cryptography is not included Additional Hashing Algorithms: CSS contains hardware accelerators for further hashing standards ( All hash functions of the SHA1 , SHA2 , SHA3 standards, the SM3 , the MD5 , the SipHash and the Poly1305 algorithm) Improved Software Support Features: CSS supports hardware context switching (by automatically assigning cryptographic engines to its 20 channels). It does not support context switching by software.

PPUC - The Parallel Processing Unit for Classic application has been added to the The PPUC offers fully programmable parallel processing capabilities based on the DesignWare EV7xFS processor IP. It includes a 32 bit Scalar Unit and a wide Vector DSP unit which can process vector elements of data type 8 bit, 16 bit or 32 bit.

LLI - Low Latency Interconnect The Low Latency Interconnect (LLI) is an Advanced eXtensible Interface (AXI) sub-system which allows simultaneous transfer between different masters and different slaves connected to it. All masters and slaves connected to the LLI have 32-bit address and data paths and operate on the same clock domain

2x Ethernet ports with bridge function enabling packet forwarding from ingress port to egress port without CPU intervention Ethernet ports support 2.5Gbps and 5Gbps Connection to external PHYs using USXGMII and SerialGMII Host interface provides 8 TX DMA and 8 RX DMA channels with up to 16 descriptor prefetching Ethernet ports provide hardware support for TSN standards IEEE 802.1Qbu Frame Preemption IEEE 802.1Qbv Enhancements for Scheduled Traffic IEEE 802.1Qci Per Stream Filtering and Policing Broadcast/multicast packet duplication supports virtualization Intrusion Detection Support with Policing Counters Automotive Safety Features Error correction code (ECC) protection for memories FSM parity and timeout protection Application/CSR interface timeout protection

HSPHY - High Speed Physical Layer The High Speed PHY block (HSPHY) is a functional block that implements physical layer blocks for Gigabit Ethernet(GETH) or Light Ethernet(LETH), PCIe, xSPI and SGBT I/O functions. It provides the infrastructure for high-speed communication interfaces The HSPHY hosts high-speed serial I/Os The HSPHY also hosts the IO control for MII, RMII, RGMII for GETH including two MDIO (Management Data Input/ Output) for both MAC0 and MAC1. It also implements the RGMII pad and skew control as well as the xSPI skew and pad control

HSSL - High Speed Serial Link The HSSL module provides point-to-point communication of both single data values and of large data blocks, called streams. The communicating partner can be complex microcontrollers, or a microcontroller and a device with only basic execution capabilities

xSPI - eXpanded Serial Peripheral Interface (xSPI) The xSPI functional block is a controller interface to enable communication with SPI slave devices compliant to the JEDEC JESD251 standard This master interface provides a low pin count connection to off-chip single, dual, quad, or octal SPI devices in SDR and DDR mode. The partner devices include SEMPER™ flash from Infineon (formerly Cypress), or Xccela from Micron and HyperBus devices such as HyperFlash (NOR flash) and HyperRAM to name a few

PCIe - Peripheral Component Interconnect Express The PCIe is a high-speed communication interface. It is a full-duplex connection capable of sending and receiving data at the same time. The interface is compliant with PCIe Gen 5 and the data rate is up to 8 GT/s.

DRE - Data Routing Engine The DRE is a hardware accelerator used to route Controller Area Network (CAN) frames and Ethernet frames The DRE routes CAN frames between Ethernet interfaces. Additionally, it can assist in routing CAN frames from one CAN interface to another CAN interface or to a user-configured memory location, and in forwarding Ethernet frames between Ethernet interfaces The DRE is aligned to the AVTP Control frame format defined by IEEE 1722-2016

New sub block CRE: Hardware accelerated CAN to CAN frame routing between nodes of one module. One module consist out of 4 nodes. Assists Data Routing Engine (DRE) to perform hardware accelerated CAN to CAN Routing between different MCMCAN modules, CAN to a user Configurable System RAM location Transfer and performs 1:4 Multi Cast Routing, that is, Routing of a received CAN frame to a maximum of up to 4 destinations Consists of an measurement unit for Intrusion Detection (IDMU) Hardware based time stamping according to CiA 603 Support of higher asynchronous frequencies, f ASYN up to 160 MHz especially for CAN FD gives more flexibility of node setups

Hardware trigger capability to request communication using a configurable timer is added in AURIX TC4xx devices When hardware trigger is enabled QSPI initiates transmission of SPI frame upon a reception of trigger signal The selection of the hardware trigger source is configurable either GTM Tx & Rx FIFO sizes increased to 8 Which was only in TC3XX

Changed maximum system clock frequency from 300 MHz to 400 MHz Changed frequency (increase) for selected peripherals and system resources System PLL: GTM, CPUs, CSRM, SPB Peripheral PLL: MSC, MCCAN Added separated oscillator watchdog for system and peripheral PLL

LBIST now based on hierarchical scan Up to 7 hierarchical scan domains depending on the family chip. Hierarchical scan domains can be scanned independently. Each domain produces its own MISR signature. Introduced LBIST scenarios: key on → LBIST used primarily to achieve required coverage on the safety relevant logic in the minimum time: 90% stuck at coverage on safety relevant logic in less than 5ms. Key-off → ” LBIST used to perform test on all digital parts within a single domain. Domains can be scanned simultaneously, independently or in a sequential way. Additional indicators have been added to reflect the LBIST configuration that led to latest LBIST reset and keep a count of potential LBIST failures.

VMT - Volatile Memory Test (Formerly known as MTU (Memory Test Unit)) The Volatile Memory Test (VMT) controls and monitors the test, initialization, and data integrity checking functions of the various internal memories in the device.

Major differences of the System Control Unit (SCU) to AURIX TC3xx Clock system and related registers are part of the CCU module. Watchdog Timer functions (WDT) are part of the WTU module. CPU Lockstep Control (LCL) is part of the CPU module. Emergency Stop function (ES) is part of the SMU module. Power Management function (PMC) and related registers are part of the SMM chapter. ESR related function and related registers are part of the SMM chapter. SCU function related to CSRM is part of the SCRM/CSCU module. Pad heating function is part of the SCR chapter. Adapted Bus APU registers to new structure (ACCEN register) Introduced local Safety Endinit Endinit /CS Endinit Control (PROTSE,PROTE, PROTCSE) ERU - Revised list of input and output connections total of 20 inputs from ports.

AURIX TC4xx supports up to 6.5 V nominal input voltage for EVRC for improved conversion efficiency at system level. Primary Voltage monitors → AURIX TC4xx monitors 20 voltage rails in comparison to AURIX TC3xx with 3 rails monitored. Secondary voltage monitors → AURIX TC4xx monitors 23 voltage rails in comparison to AURIX TC3xx with 6 rails monitored. Standby domain - AURIX TC4xx supports multiple encapsulated standby domains for better tradeoff between low quiescent current targets and available functionality. AURIX TC3xx supports only one standby domain. Standby RAM - Up to 256 kB standby RAM in the CPU ( DLMU ) and 32 kB standby RAM (XRAM) in the SCR in comparison to AURIX™ TC3xx with up to 128 kB and 32 kB accordingly. ![(plus)](https://inside-docupedia.bosch.com/confluence/s/-3dmz00/9114/1yg1r0c/_/images/icons/emoticons/add.svg) RTC - AURIX TC4xx has been added RTC and timer with external 32 kHz crystal support and temperature trimming.

The VTMON module interfaces to distributed temperature monitors at different locations across the microcontroller. Central VTMON instance added to co ordinate data transfer to distributed monitor instances. 4 new DTS instances added. Thermal Shut Down alarm and reset possibility added in addition to earlier high and low temperature alarms.

Maximum core frequency changed from 20 MHz to 100 MHz XRAM data and code memory changed from 8 kB to 32 kB Boot ROM memory changed from 2 kB to 4 kB Changed pinning Number of standby pins: from 16 to 26 Number of 8 bit ports: from 2 to 4 Default ownership changed of shared ports granted to SCR if SCR is enabled Added STBY0 /1 states; transitions initiate able from SCR software Added second interrupt line from SCR to the main SoC Added I2C module to control external power supply ICs

Added a lockstep IR functionality The IR implements a lockstep mechanism using a redundant checker core for safety. The maximum number of Service Request Nodes (SRN) is increased to 2048 Added a CPU Hardware Virtualization support: Each SRN can be mapped to one VM Modified Service Request Enable (SRE) bit: Modified Service Request Enable (SRE) bit: The mapping of SRC registers to the IR address map has been modified

Local safety ENDINIT replaced by PROT mechanism ( SEy , CSE, SSE) Access to safety ENDINIT protected register using safety ENDINIT protection mechanism, implemented with WDT control register, is replaced with module specific PROT mechanism, implemented with module specific PROT register sets to provide ability to assign ownership of a set of registers to a PROT owner. Generation of global ENDINIT and safety ENDINIT discontinued. Safety watchdog removed and one system and one security watchdog added. New system watchdog is not fixed to a certain CPU. Security watchdog is designated to CPUcs , inside CSRM. Added bus APU protection for ‘read’ and assignment to one or more virtual machines. WDTCPU0 and WDTSYS are enabled by boot FW while others are disabled after reset.

Safety alarms handling instance SMU_CORE has been renamed as SMU_SAFE0 A second redundant safety alarm handling instance named SMU_SAFE1 has been added Allows redundancy on the Fault Signaling Protocol FSP output Allows handling of multiple safety applications with different alarm reactions Handling of cybersecurity related alarms has been added An alarm handling instance for cybersecurity alarms named SMU_CS has been added Full name changed from “Safety Management Unit” to “Safety and Security Alarm Management Unit”
Added shadow register functionality to ATOM channel for CLK_SRC and SL in AURIX  TC4xx by adding a new register called CLSi_ATOM_CHx_CTRL_SR Many changes to MCS - which we don't care

Enhanced Generic Timer Module eGTM is an enhanced version of the GTM, a universal timer architecture provided by Bosch AE The eGTM is capable of generating complex PWM/Pulses as for example motor control or power conversion. The module can be used for input signal characterization and for many other use cases - support electric drive application requirements

Added new CDSP (Converter Digital Signal Processing) module CDSP module can be used for digital post processing any ADC output Removed EVADC module of AURIX TC3xx and added new TMADC Time Multiplexed Analog to Digital Converter ) module instead TMADC has two conversion cores and 16 analog inputs + 2 monitoring inputs TMADC supports up to 4 MSPs output data rate Removed Fast compare channel module of AURIX TC3xx and added new FCC (Fast Compare Comparator) module instead FCC supports an output data rate of up to 40 Mbps Removed converter control block CONVCTRL of AURIX TC3xx Removed the EDSADC of AURIX™ TC3xx and added two independent modules DSADC and EXMOD DSADC supports on chip analog modulator and decimation filter EXMOD supports only decimation filter to demodulate the external modulator signal Number of samples which can be accumulated has been increased to 128 from 64 in AURIX TC3xx

Extend support of alternative functions per pin from 8 to 16 Extended options on driver strength and voltage level Extended reaction options on emergency stop Added read back of HWSEL status Introduced Pad Configuration register PADCFG IOCR and PDR registers have been removed ACCEN register has been removed Add new feature to locally protect pad configuration and pin levels in access groups Readback of LVDS values possible Almost all ports have configurability for Emergency Stop (like push, pull, Open Drain)

No Checker Software anymore AURIXTC4xx start up for safety applications is assured by Firmware of ASIL D supporting the safety relevant features. The control of LBIST execution during start up is separated from Boot Mode Header data structures. BMHD.LBISTENA bit removed. Control has been moved from boot mode header to UCB. UCB0_17.LBISTEXE added. The control of Lockstep disable is separated from Boot Mode Header data structures. BMHD.LSENA0 to BMHD.LSENA3 bits removed. Control has been moved from boot mode header to UCB. UCB0_17.LOCKSTEP_ena added. Stand by Controller is not anymore started by Firmware user code must take care.

Optimized SRI interconnect with more segments GTM and ADC resources split onto own FPI segment CPB Low Latency Interconnect added (LLI) Enhanced access protection mechanisms PROT mechanism provides the ability to assign ownership of resources to single master agent functions Access Protection Unit (APU) enhances the AURIX TC3xx ACCEN mechanism to restrict read and write access to registers or memory Access protection can now be configured for virtual machine identifiers (VM) or protection register sets Enhanced SOTA support SOTA can be enabled or disabled for application PFLASH and PFLASHcs independently All available application PFLASH can be used in SOTA mode Read While Write (RWW) capability available for application PFLASH update PFLASHcs supports a restricted form of SOTA without RWW support No duplication of secure application code necessary
